title: dactylorhiza maculata subsp. sudetica
intro: Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. sudetica, also known as the spotted marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid found in Europe, particularly in mountainous regions. The medicinal properties of Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. sudetica are primarily attributed to its furostanol saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The health benefits of this plant are said to include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. sudetica can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its medicinal use is not widely established and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
